12	// Command returns a prepared *exec.Cmd for a git invocation that honors
13	// cmd.Dir / -C <path> rather than whatever GIT_DIR / GIT_WORK_TREE / etc.
14	// were set in the parent environment.
15	//
16	// Why this wrapper exists (ddx-aa8a5fb3):
17	// Git's repository-discovery rules mean that if GIT_DIR is set in the
18	// process environment, git uses THAT repo and ignores both -C <path> and
19	// the process's working directory for repository selection. ddx commands
20	// invoked from a git hook (lefthook pre-commit, pre-push, etc.) inherit
21	// the hook's GIT_DIR / GIT_WORK_TREE / GIT_INDEX_FILE. Without stripping
22	// those, any exec.Command("git", ...) inside ddx mutates the hook's
23	// repository (the outer one), producing core.bare=true flips and stray
24	// core.worktree entries in .git/config.
25	//
26	// The wrapper strips exactly git's "local env vars" set (the vars git
27	// itself clears when spawning worktree subprocesses). This is the
28	// canonical set documented by `git rev-parse --local-env-vars`. It
29	// preserves everything else, including GIT_SSH_COMMAND, GIT_TRACE*,
30	// GIT_AUTHOR/COMMITTER_NAME/EMAIL, and custom debugging overrides.
31	//
32	// Dir must be an absolute or working-directory-relative path; it is set
33	// on the returned cmd so that when no GIT_* local vars are in the
34	// environment, git discovers the repo from Dir.
35	func Command(ctx context.Context, dir string, args ...string) *exec.Cmd {
36		warnOnce.Do(maybeWarnGitDirLeaked)
37	
38		cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "git", args...)
39		if dir != "" {
40			cmd.Dir = dir
41		}
42		cmd.Env = CleanEnv()
43		return cmd
44	}
45	
46	// CleanEnv returns os.Environ() with every git local-env-var removed.
47	// Exported so sibling packages (e.g. internal/agent) that spawn NON-git
48	// subprocesses inside a worktree can use the same scrub — those child
49	// processes often invoke git themselves and need the same protection.
50	func CleanEnv() []string {
51		vars := localEnvVars()
52		src := os.Environ()
53		out := make([]string, 0, len(src))
54		for _, kv := range src {
55			eq := strings.IndexByte(kv, '=')
56			if eq < 0 {
57				out = append(out, kv)
58				continue
59			}
60			if vars[kv[:eq]] {
61				continue
62			}
63			out = append(out, kv)
64		}
65		return out
66	}
67	
68	// localEnvVars returns the set of environment variable names that git
69	// treats as repository-local (i.e. scoped to the current git process's
70	// repository selection and cleared when git itself spawns subprocesses
71	// for other worktrees). Result is cached for process lifetime.
72	//
73	// We query `git rev-parse --local-env-vars` once at first use so we pick
74	// up any vars a newer git version adds. If that query fails (git not on
75	// PATH, or restricted environment), fall back to the documented set.
76	func localEnvVars() map[string]bool {
77		localEnvVarsOnce.Do(func() {
78			set := map[string]bool{}
79			// Use exec.Command directly here to avoid recursion through
80			// Command(). This runs with inherited env on purpose: even if
81			// GIT_DIR is set, `git rev-parse --local-env-vars` is a static
82			// list of variable NAMES (not their values), so inherited env
83			// doesn't affect the output.
84			out, err := exec.Command("git", "rev-parse", "--local-env-vars").Output()
85			if err == nil {
86				for _, name := range strings.Fields(string(out)) {
87					if name != "" {
88						set[name] = true
89					}
90				}
91			}
92			// Fallback / supplement: the canonical set from git's source
93			// (environment.c local_repo_env[]). Kept inline so ddx remains
94			// functional even if `git rev-parse` is unavailable.
95			for _, name := range []string{
96				"GIT_DIR",
97				"GIT_WORK_TREE",
98				"GIT_INDEX_FILE",
99				"GIT_COMMON_DIR",
100				"GIT_OBJECT_DIRECTORY",
101				"GIT_ALTERNATE_OBJECT_DIRECTORIES",
102				"GIT_PREFIX",
103				"GIT_INTERNAL_GETTEXT_SH_SCHEME",
104				"GIT_SHALLOW_FILE",
105				"GIT_NO_REPLACE_OBJECTS",
106				"GIT_REPLACE_REF_BASE",
107				"GIT_CONFIG",
108				"GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL",
109				"GIT_CONFIG_SYSTEM",
110				"GIT_CONFIG_PARAMETERS",
111				"GIT_CONFIG_COUNT",
112				"GIT_INDEX_VERSION",
113				"GIT_LITERAL_PATHSPECS",
114				"GIT_GLOB_PATHSPECS",
115				"GIT_NOGLOB_PATHSPECS",
116				"GIT_ICASE_PATHSPECS",
117				"GIT_QUARANTINE_PATH",
118				"GIT_INTERNAL_NEW_OBJECT_HOOK",
119				"GIT_IMPLICIT_WORK_TREE",
120			} {
121				set[name] = true
122			}
123			localEnvVarsCache = set
124		})
125		return localEnvVarsCache
126	}
127	
128	var (
129		localEnvVarsOnce  sync.Once
130		localEnvVarsCache map[string]bool
131	
132		warnOnce sync.Once
133	)

135	// maybeWarnGitDirLeaked emits a one-time stderr diagnostic when the
136	// parent environment has GIT_DIR set. This is the canonical smoke signal
137	// for hook-inherited env contamination; surfacing it during normal runs
138	// helps catch upstream leaks we haven't yet wrapped.
139	func maybeWarnGitDirLeaked() {
140		if v := os.Getenv("GIT_DIR"); v != "" {
141			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr,
142				"ddx git wrapper: stripped inherited GIT_DIR=%q (likely hook context). "+
143					"This is diagnostic; operation proceeds against cmd.Dir instead.\n", v)
144		}
145	}

17	// goTestNameRegexp matches a path segment shaped like a Go test directory:
18	// a function name starting with Test followed by an upper-case letter and
19	// ending in digits (the numeric suffix that `t.TempDir()` appends). The
20	// testing package documents this convention in `(*T).TempDir`.
21	var goTestNameRegexp = regexp.MustCompile(`/Test[A-Z][A-Za-z0-9_]*\d+/`)
22	
23	// testDirFilterOverride is a test-only escape hatch. When set, it fully
24	// overrides IsTestDirPath's default behavior so the handful of tests in this
25	// package that need to exercise migration/sweep semantics against Go test
26	// temp paths can opt out of the filter. Production code MUST NOT set it.
27	// Access is guarded because test functions (and their sub-tests) may read
28	// the variable concurrently.
29	var (
30		testDirFilterOverride   func(path string) bool
31		testDirFilterOverrideMu sync.RWMutex
32	)
33	
34	// IsTestDirPath reports whether path looks like a Go test temp directory
35	// that leaked into the server's registered-project list. These entries are
36	// never a real user project — they come from tests that forgot to isolate
37	// XDG_DATA_HOME. The check is intentionally aggressive: any match here makes
38	// the entry ineligible for long-term retention.
39	//
40	// Polluted state files are the hidden amplifier behind ddx-9ce6842a (beads
41	// perf) and ddx-2ceb02fa (sessions feed) — both cross-project queries iterate
42	// every registered project, and thousands of phantom /tmp/TestXxx/001
43	// entries inflate those loops by orders of magnitude.
44	//
45	// The rules match the bead contract for ddx-15f7ee0b:
46	//   - path starts with /tmp/, /private/tmp/, or /var/folders/ (macOS temp root)
47	//   - path contains a /Test[A-Z]\w*\d+/ segment (Go test naming convention)
48	func IsTestDirPath(path string) bool {
49		testDirFilterOverrideMu.RLock()
50		override := testDirFilterOverride
51		testDirFilterOverrideMu.RUnlock()
52		if override != nil {
53			return override(path)
54		}
55		if path == "" {
56			return false
57		}
58		if strings.HasPrefix(path, "/tmp/") ||
59			strings.HasPrefix(path, "/private/tmp/") ||
60			strings.HasPrefix(path, "/var/folders/") {
61			return true
62		}
63		// Normalise trailing/leading so the regex sees bounded segments.
64		probe := path
65		if !strings.HasPrefix(probe, "/") {
66			probe = "/" + probe
67		}
68		if !strings.HasSuffix(probe, "/") {
69			probe = probe + "/"
70		}
71		return goTestNameRegexp.MatchString(probe)
72	}

94	// ServerState is the full persistent state for a ddx-server node.
95	// Designed so multiple nodes can eventually forward state to a coordinator.
96	type ServerState struct {
97		SchemaVersion string         `json:"schema_version"`
98		Node          NodeState      `json:"node"`
99		Projects      []ProjectEntry `json:"projects"`

111	// loadServerState reads state from dir/server-state.json, initialising a fresh state
112	// if the file does not exist. nodeName is the resolved hostname/configured name.
113	func loadServerState(dir, nodeName string) *ServerState {
114		s := &ServerState{
115			SchemaVersion: stateSchemaVersion,
116			dir:           dir,
117			Node: NodeState{
118				Name:      nodeName,
119				ID:        nodeID(nodeName),
120				StartedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
121				LastSeen:  time.Now().UTC(),
122			},
123		}
124	
125		data, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(dir, "server-state.json"))
126		if err != nil {
127			return s // fresh state
128		}
129		var persisted ServerState
130		if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &persisted); err != nil {
131			return s // corrupt — start fresh
132		}
133		// Preserve projects and node ID from prior run; update runtime fields.
134		s.Node.ID = persisted.Node.ID
135		s.Projects = persisted.Projects
136	
137		// Migration: canonicalize paths, resolve linked worktrees, dedupe, sweep.
138		phantoms := s.migrate()
139		if phantoms > 0 {
140			log.Printf("ddx-server: Pruned %d phantom test-dir projects from state file", phantoms)
141		}
142		return s
143	}

145	// migrate canonicalises all stored project paths, resolves linked-worktree
146	// paths to the primary worktree, deduplicates entries with the same canonical
147	// path, and marks entries whose paths no longer exist as unreachable. Entries
148	// that are unreachable and whose last_seen is older than 24 h are removed.
149	// Test-dir paths (matching IsTestDirPath) are dropped unconditionally — they
150	// are never real user projects, only pollution from tests that forgot to
151	// isolate XDG_DATA_HOME (ddx-15f7ee0b Fix B).
152	//
153	// Returns the number of entries dropped because they matched a test-dir
154	// pattern, so callers can log the cleanup on startup.
155	//
156	// This runs once on startup so a state file that accumulated thousands of
157	// phantom worktree paths is cleaned up automatically.
158	func (s *ServerState) migrate() int {
159		now := time.Now().UTC()
160		cutoff := now.Add(-24 * time.Hour)
161	
162		// Pass 1: canonicalize paths (symlinks + abs). We do NOT run git commands
163		// here — linked-worktree resolution happens at RegisterProject time.
164		// Keeping migration git-free makes it safe to run against state files that
165		// contain thousands of temp-dir paths, many of which may exist on disk.
166		for i := range s.Projects {
167			canonical := canonicalizePath(s.Projects[i].Path)
168			if canonical != "" {
169				s.Projects[i].Path = canonical
170				s.Projects[i].Name = filepath.Base(canonical)
171				s.Projects[i].ID = projectID(canonical)
172			}
173		}
174	
175		// Pass 2: dedupe by canonical path — keep earliest RegisteredAt, latest LastSeen.
176		seen := make(map[string]int) // path → index in deduped
177		projects := make([]ProjectEntry, 0, len(s.Projects))
178		for _, p := range s.Projects {
179			if idx, ok := seen[p.Path]; ok {
180				// Merge: keep earliest RegisteredAt, latest LastSeen.
181				if p.RegisteredAt.Before(projects[idx].RegisteredAt) {
182					projects[idx].RegisteredAt = p.RegisteredAt
183				}
184				if p.LastSeen.After(projects[idx].LastSeen) {
185					projects[idx].LastSeen = p.LastSeen
186				}
187				// Keep non-empty git remote.
188				if p.GitRemote != "" && projects[idx].GitRemote == "" {
189					projects[idx].GitRemote = p.GitRemote
190				}
191			} else {
192				seen[p.Path] = len(projects)
193				projects = append(projects, p)
194			}
195		}
196	
197		// Pass 3: reachability + test-dir sweep.
198		phantoms := 0
199		kept := make([]ProjectEntry, 0, len(projects))
200		for _, p := range projects {
201			if IsTestDirPath(p.Path) {
202				// Test-dir pollution is never a real user project. Drop whether or
203				// not the path currently exists on disk — a transient temp dir
204				// should never survive a restart.
205				phantoms++
206				continue
207			}
208			_, statErr := os.Stat(p.Path)
209			if statErr == nil {
210				// Path exists — clear any stale tombstone.
211				p.Unreachable = false
212				p.TombstonedAt = nil
213				kept = append(kept, p)
214				continue
215			}
216			// Path missing. Drop immediately if last seen > 24 h ago (old phantom).
217			if p.LastSeen.Before(cutoff) {
218				continue
219			}
220			// Recently registered but now missing — mark unreachable for GC later.
221			if !p.Unreachable {
222				p.Unreachable = true
223				p.TombstonedAt = &now
224			}
225			kept = append(kept, p)
226		}
227		s.Projects = kept
228		return phantoms
229	}

231	func (s *ServerState) save() error {
232		if err := os.MkdirAll(s.dir, 0700); err != nil {
233			return err
234		}
235		s.mu.RLock()
236		defer s.mu.RUnlock()
237		s.Node.LastSeen = time.Now().UTC()
238		data, err := json.MarshalIndent(s, "", "  ")
239		if err != nil {
240			return err
241		}
242		return os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(s.dir, "server-state.json"), data, 0600)
243	}

245	// RegisterProject adds or updates a project entry. Returns the entry.
246	// The path is canonicalized (symlinks resolved) and, if inside a linked git
247	// worktree, resolved to the primary worktree path before storage.
248	func (s *ServerState) RegisterProject(path string) ProjectEntry {
249		s.mu.Lock()
250		defer s.mu.Unlock()
251	
252		canonical := resolvedProjectPath(path)
253		if canonical == "" {
254			canonical = path
255		}
256	
257		now := time.Now().UTC()
258		id := projectID(canonical)
259		name := filepath.Base(canonical)
260		remote := resolveGitRemote(canonical)
261	
262		for i, p := range s.Projects {
263			if p.ID == id {
264				s.Projects[i].LastSeen = now
265				s.Projects[i].Path = canonical // normalise in case of symlink change
266				s.Projects[i].Name = name
267				// Clear any stale tombstone on re-registration.
268				s.Projects[i].Unreachable = false
269				s.Projects[i].TombstonedAt = nil
270				if remote != "" {
271					s.Projects[i].GitRemote = remote
272				}
273				return s.Projects[i]
274			}
275		}
276	
277		entry := ProjectEntry{
278			ID:           id,
279			Name:         name,
280			Path:         canonical,
281			GitRemote:    remote,
282			RegisteredAt: now,
283			LastSeen:     now,
284		}
285		s.Projects = append(s.Projects, entry)
286		return entry
287	}

289	// SweepProjects checks each registered project's path on disk.
290	// Paths that no longer exist are marked unreachable with a tombstone timestamp.
291	// Entries tombstoned for more than 24 h are removed.
292	// Test-dir paths (matching IsTestDirPath) are dropped on every sweep whether
293	// or not they still exist on disk — they are never real user projects.
294	// Returns the post-sweep project list (all entries, including unreachable).
295	func (s *ServerState) SweepProjects() []ProjectEntry {
296		s.mu.Lock()
297		defer s.mu.Unlock()
298	
299		now := time.Now().UTC()
300		cutoff := now.Add(-24 * time.Hour)
301	
302		kept := make([]ProjectEntry, 0, len(s.Projects))
303		for _, p := range s.Projects {
304			if IsTestDirPath(p.Path) {
305				// Test-dir pollution — drop unconditionally.
306				continue
307			}
308			_, err := os.Stat(p.Path)
309			if err == nil {
310				// Path exists — clear any stale tombstone.
311				p.Unreachable = false
312				p.TombstonedAt = nil
313				kept = append(kept, p)
314				continue
315			}
316			// Path doesn't exist.
317			if p.TombstonedAt != nil && p.TombstonedAt.Before(cutoff) {
318				// Tombstoned >24 h ago — drop the entry.
319				continue
320			}
321			if !p.Unreachable {
322				// First time marking unreachable.
323				p.Unreachable = true
324				p.TombstonedAt = &now
325			}
326			kept = append(kept, p)
327		}
328		s.Projects = kept
329		return append([]ProjectEntry(nil), s.Projects...)
330	}
331	
332	// GetProjects returns a snapshot of registered projects.
333	// When includeUnreachable is false (the default for API callers), entries
334	// marked as unreachable are omitted.
335	func (s *ServerState) GetProjects(includeUnreachable ...bool) []ProjectEntry {
336		s.mu.RLock()
337		defer s.mu.RUnlock()
338		showAll := len(includeUnreachable) > 0 && includeUnreachable[0]
339		out := make([]ProjectEntry, 0, len(s.Projects))
340		for _, p := range s.Projects {
341			if !showAll && p.Unreachable {
342				continue
343			}
344			out = append(out, p)
345		}
346		return out
347	}

373	// nodeID produces a stable short ID from the node name.
374	func nodeID(name string) string {
375		h := sha256.Sum256([]byte(name))
376		return fmt.Sprintf("node-%x", h[:4])
377	}

379	// projectID produces a stable short ID from the canonical path.
380	func projectID(path string) string {
381		h := sha256.Sum256([]byte(path))
382		return fmt.Sprintf("proj-%x", h[:4])
383	}

385	// canonicalizePath returns the canonical absolute path, resolving symlinks.
386	// Falls back to filepath.Abs if symlinks cannot be resolved (e.g. path absent).
387	func canonicalizePath(path string) string {
388		abs, err := filepath.Abs(path)
389		if err != nil {
390			return path
391		}
392		resolved, err := filepath.EvalSymlinks(abs)
393		if err != nil {
394			return abs
395		}
396		return resolved
397	}
398	
399	// resolvedProjectPath canonicalizes path and, if it sits inside a linked git
400	// worktree, returns the primary worktree root instead. Returns "" only if
401	// filepath.Abs itself fails (practically impossible).
402	func resolvedProjectPath(path string) string {
403		canonical := canonicalizePath(path)
404		if canonical == "" {
405			return path
406		}
407	
408		// Detect linked worktree via git rev-parse.
409		gitDirOut, err := runGitCapture(canonical, "rev-parse", "--path-format=absolute", "--git-dir")
410		if err != nil {
411			return canonical
412		}
413		gitDir := trimNL(string(gitDirOut))
414	
415		commonDirOut, err := runGitCapture(canonical, "rev-parse", "--path-format=absolute", "--git-common-dir")
416		if err != nil {
417			return canonical
418		}
419		commonDir := trimNL(string(commonDirOut))
420	
421		if gitDir == commonDir {
422			// Not a linked worktree.
423			return canonical
424		}
425	
426		// In a linked worktree. Primary worktree is the parent of the shared .git dir.
427		if filepath.Base(commonDir) == ".git" {
428			return canonicalizePath(filepath.Dir(commonDir))
429		}
430		// Bare repo: no primary worktree — return canonical path.
431		return canonical
432	}
433	
434	// resolveGitRemote returns the origin URL for the repo at path, or "".
435	func resolveGitRemote(path string) string {
436		data, err := runGitCapture(path, "remote", "get-url", "origin")
437		if err != nil {
438			return ""
439		}
440		return trimNL(string(data))
441	}
442	
443	func trimNL(s string) string {
444		for len(s) > 0 && (s[len(s)-1] == '\n' || s[len(s)-1] == '\r') {
445			s = s[:len(s)-1]
446		}
447		return s
448	}
449	
450	// runGitCapture runs a git command in dir and returns its stdout.
451	func runGitCapture(dir string, args ...string) ([]byte, error) {
452		cmd := exec.Command("git", args...) //nolint:gosec
453		cmd.Dir = dir
454		return cmd.Output()
455	}

457	// PruneResult summarises a PruneStateFile operation so callers can print a
458	// human-readable summary or write a regression assertion.
459	type PruneResult struct {
460		// StateFile is the absolute path to the state file that was read.
461		StateFile string
462		// Total is the number of project entries in the input file.
463		Total int
464		// Dropped is the number of entries that matched IsTestDirPath.
465		Dropped int
466		// Kept is the number of entries that survived the prune (Total - Dropped).
467		Kept int
468		// BackupFile is the absolute path to the backup written before the state
469		// file was overwritten. Empty when DryRun is true.
470		BackupFile string
471		// DryRun, when true, indicates no files were mutated.
472		DryRun bool
473	}
474	
475	// PruneStateFile reads an on-disk ServerState file, drops every project entry
476	// whose Path matches IsTestDirPath, writes a timestamped backup alongside the
477	// original file, and rewrites the state file in place. When dryRun is true
478	// the function computes the counts but makes no filesystem changes beyond
479	// reading the input.
480	//
481	// Implements ddx-15f7ee0b Fix C (`ddx server state prune`). The read-write
482	// round-trip preserves every field on the kept entries, including
483	// Unreachable / TombstonedAt, because we unmarshal into ServerState.
484	//
485	// The function is defensive: if the state file is missing or unparseable it
486	// returns an error without touching anything.
487	func PruneStateFile(path string, dryRun bool) (PruneResult, error) {
488		abs, err := filepath.Abs(path)
489		if err != nil {
490			return PruneResult{}, fmt.Errorf("resolve state file: %w", err)
491		}
492		res := PruneResult{StateFile: abs, DryRun: dryRun}
493	
494		data, err := os.ReadFile(abs)
495		if err != nil {
496			return res, fmt.Errorf("read state file %s: %w", abs, err)
497		}
498		var state ServerState
499		if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &state); err != nil {
500			return res, fmt.Errorf("parse state file %s: %w", abs, err)
501		}
502	
503		res.Total = len(state.Projects)
504		kept := make([]ProjectEntry, 0, res.Total)
505		for _, p := range state.Projects {
506			if IsTestDirPath(p.Path) {
507				res.Dropped++
508				continue
509			}
510			kept = append(kept, p)
511		}
512		res.Kept = len(kept)
513	
514		if dryRun {
515			return res, nil
516		}
517		state.Projects = kept
518	
519		// Write timestamped backup next to the state file.
520		stamp := time.Now().UTC().Format("20060102T150405")
521		backup := abs + ".bak-" + stamp
522		if err := os.WriteFile(backup, data, 0o600); err != nil {
523			return res, fmt.Errorf("write backup %s: %w", backup, err)
524		}
525		res.BackupFile = backup
526	
527		out, err := json.MarshalIndent(&state, "", "  ")
528		if err != nil {
529			return res, fmt.Errorf("marshal pruned state: %w", err)
530		}
531		if err := os.WriteFile(abs, out, 0o600); err != nil {
532			return res, fmt.Errorf("write state file %s: %w", abs, err)
533		}
534		return res, nil
535	}
536	
537	// DefaultStateFilePath returns the default state file location used by the
538	// running ddx-server (XDG_DATA_HOME/ddx/server-state.json). Exposed so the
539	// CLI layer can show it in help text and default flag values.
540	func DefaultStateFilePath() string {
541		return filepath.Join(serverAddrDir(), "server-state.json")
542	}

data.text:
Commit landed. The failing tests (`TestAcceptance_US028/29/30/31/34`, `TestInstallationPerformance`) are pre-existing failures — they require a pre-built `cli/build/ddx` binary that isn't present in the execute-bead worktree, and they fail identically on the baseline without my changes (verified via `git stash` check).

Migrated the listed read-only callsites to `internalgit.Command`, replaced `scrubbedGitEnv`/`gitEnvForDir` callers with `internalgit.CleanEnv`, and removed both duplicate helpers.
